load_config_file.description: Whether to load YAML/JSON CMS configuration file(s) when [manually initializing the CMS](https://sveltiacms.app/en/docs/api/initialization). This works only in the `CMS.init()` method’s `config` option. Default: `true`.

media_folder.markdownDescription: Global internal media folder path, relative to the project’s root directory. Required unless a cloud media storage is configured.

config.description: Options to be passed to Cloudinary, such as `multiple`. The `cloud_name` and `api_key` options are required for the global `media_library` option. See the [Cloudinary documentation](https://cloudinary.com/documentation/media_library_widget#2_set_the_configuration_options) for a full list of available options. Some options, including `inline_container`, will be ignored in Sveltia CMS because we use an API-based integration instead of Cloudinary’s pre-built widget.

Some options, including `inline_container`, will be ignored in Sveltia CMS because we use an API-based integration instead of Cloudinary’s pre-built widget.

name.description: Library name.
name.markdownDescription: Library name.

config.description: Options to be passed to Uploadcare, such as `multiple`. The `publicKey` option is required for the global `media_library` option. Some options, including `previewStep`, will be ignored in Sveltia CMS because we use an API-based integration instead of Uploadcare’s deprecated jQuery File Uploader.

name.description: Media library name (used when configuring via legacy `media_library`).
name.markdownDescription: Media library name (used when configuring via legacy `media_library`).

access_key_id.description: AWS access key ID or equivalent (safe to store in config).
access_key_id.markdownDescription: AWS access key ID or equivalent (safe to store in config).

region.description: AWS region (e.g., 'us-east-1'). Required for Amazon S3, DigitalOcean Spaces, Scaleway Object Storage, and Supabase Storage.
region.markdownDescription: AWS region (e.g., 'us-east-1'). Required for Amazon S3, DigitalOcean Spaces, Scaleway Object Storage, and Supabase Storage.

account_id.description: Cloudflare account ID. Required for Cloudflare R2.
account_id.markdownDescription: Cloudflare account ID. Required for Cloudflare R2.

jurisdiction.description: Cloudflare R2 jurisdiction. Required for buckets created in the EU or FedRAMP jurisdictions; the global endpoint returns an error for those buckets. Default: `'default'`.
jurisdiction.markdownDescription: Cloudflare R2 jurisdiction. Required for buckets created in the EU or FedRAMP jurisdictions; the global endpoint returns an error for those buckets. Default: `'default'`.

project_id.description: Supabase project reference ID. Required for Supabase Storage.
project_id.markdownDescription: Supabase project reference ID. Required for Supabase Storage.

endpoint.description: Custom endpoint URL for S3-compatible services.
endpoint.markdownDescription: Custom endpoint URL for S3-compatible services.

force_path_style.description: Use path-style URLs instead of virtual-hosted-style.
force_path_style.markdownDescription: Use path-style URLs instead of virtual-hosted-style.

public_url.description: Base URL for public asset access. When set, asset preview and download URLs are constructed as `{public_url}/{key}` instead of the S3 API endpoint URL. Required for Cloudflare R2 (S3 API endpoint always requires authentication); set to the `r2.dev` development URL (e.g. `https://pub-abcd1234.r2.dev`) or a custom domain. Optional for Amazon S3 and DigitalOcean Spaces — use when serving assets through a CDN or custom domain (e.g. CloudFront or Route 53 for S3, CDN endpoint for Spaces).
public_url.markdownDescription: Base URL for public asset access. When set, asset preview and download URLs are constructed as `{public_url}/{key}` instead of the S3 API endpoint URL. Required for Cloudflare R2 (S3 API endpoint always requires authentication); set to the `r2.dev` development URL (e.g. `https://pub-abcd1234.r2.dev`) or a custom domain. Optional for Amazon S3 and DigitalOcean Spaces — use when serving assets through a CDN or custom domain (e.g. CloudFront or Route 53 for S3, CDN endpoint for Spaces).

media_library.markdownDescription: Legacy media storage option that allows only one library. This overrides the global `media_library` option. Use `media_libraries` instead to support multiple storage providers.

media_libraries.description: Unified media storage option that supports multiple libraries. See the [documentation](https://sveltiacms.app/en/docs/media#configuration) for details.
media_libraries.markdownDescription: Unified media storage option that supports multiple libraries. See the [documentation](https://sveltiacms.app/en/docs/media#configuration) for details.

app_title.markdownDescription: Custom title for the CMS, which will be displayed on the login page and the browser’s tab. Default: `Sveltia CMS`.

autoFilename.description: Whether to append a file name to an output URL. Default: `false`.
autoFilename.markdownDescription: Whether to append a file name to an output URL. Default: `false`.

defaultOperations.description: Transformation operations to be included in an output URL. Default: empty string.
defaultOperations.markdownDescription: Transformation operations to be included in an output URL. Default: empty string.

StockAssetProviderName.description: Name of supported stock photo/video provider.
StockAssetProviderName.markdownDescription: Name of supported stock photo/video provider.

all.description: Default options that apply to all internal and cloud media libraries, except for Cloudinary, which uses its own widget. These options can be overridden by library-specific options.
all.markdownDescription: Default options that apply to all internal and cloud media libraries, except for Cloudinary, which uses its own widget. These options can be overridden by library-specific options.

default.description: Options for the default media storage. Set to `false` to explicitly disable the default (internal) storage.
default.markdownDescription: Options for the default media storage. Set to `false` to explicitly disable the default (internal) storage.

cloudinary.description: Options for the Cloudinary media storage. Set to `false` to explicitly disable.
cloudinary.markdownDescription: Options for the Cloudinary media storage. Set to `false` to explicitly disable.

uploadcare.description: Options for the Uploadcare media storage. Set to `false` to explicitly disable.
uploadcare.markdownDescription: Options for the Uploadcare media storage. Set to `false` to explicitly disable.

aws_s3.description: Options for the Amazon S3 media storage. Set to `false` to explicitly disable.
aws_s3.markdownDescription: Options for the Amazon S3 media storage. Set to `false` to explicitly disable.

cloudflare_r2.description: Options for the Cloudflare R2 media storage. Set to `false` to explicitly disable.
cloudflare_r2.markdownDescription: Options for the Cloudflare R2 media storage. Set to `false` to explicitly disable.

digitalocean_spaces.description: Options for the DigitalOcean Spaces media storage. Set to `false` to explicitly disable.
digitalocean_spaces.markdownDescription: Options for the DigitalOcean Spaces media storage. Set to `false` to explicitly disable.

scaleway_object_storage.description: Options for the Scaleway Object Storage media storage. Set to `false` to explicitly disable.
scaleway_object_storage.markdownDescription: Options for the Scaleway Object Storage media storage. Set to `false` to explicitly disable.

supabase_storage.description: Options for the Supabase Storage media storage. Set to `false` to explicitly disable.
supabase_storage.markdownDescription: Options for the Supabase Storage media storage. Set to `false` to explicitly disable.

stock_assets.description: Options for the unified stock photo/video media library. Set to `false` to explicitly disable.
stock_assets.markdownDescription: Options for the unified stock photo/video media library. Set to `false` to explicitly disable.

MediaLibraries.description: Unified media storage option that supports multiple storage providers. See the [documentation](https://sveltiacms.app/en/docs/media#configuration) for details.
MediaLibraries.markdownDescription: Unified media storage option that supports multiple storage providers. See the [documentation](https://sveltiacms.app/en/docs/media#configuration) for details.

max_file_size.description: Maximum file size in bytes that can be accepted for uploading.
max_file_size.markdownDescription: Maximum file size in bytes that can be accepted for uploading.

slugify_filename.description: Whether to rename an original asset file when saving it, according to the global `slug` option. Default: `false`, meaning that the original file name is kept by default, while Netlify/Decap CMS forces to slugify file names. If set to `true`, for example, `Hello World (1).webp` would be `hello-world-1.webp`.
slugify_filename.markdownDescription: Whether to rename an original asset file when saving it, according to the global `slug` option. Default: `false`, meaning that the original file name is kept by default, while Netlify/Decap CMS forces to slugify file names. If set to `true`, for example, `Hello World (1).webp` would be `hello-world-1.webp`.

transformations.description: File transformation option map. The key is an original format like `png` or `jpeg`. It can also be `raster_image` that matches any supported raster image format. See the [documentation](https://sveltiacms.app/en/docs/media/internal#image-optimization) for details.
transformations.markdownDescription: File transformation option map. The key is an original format like `png` or `jpeg`. It can also be `raster_image` that matches any supported raster image format. See the [documentation](https://sveltiacms.app/en/docs/media/internal#image-optimization) for details.

SharedMediaLibraryOptions.description: Shared options that apply to all media libraries.
SharedMediaLibraryOptions.markdownDescription: Shared options that apply to all media libraries.

config.description: Configuration for the default media storage.
config.markdownDescription: Configuration for the default media storage.

DefaultMediaLibrary.description: Options for the default media storage.
DefaultMediaLibrary.markdownDescription: Options for the default media storage.

output_filename_only.description: Whether to output a file name instead of a full URL. Default: `false`.
output_filename_only.markdownDescription: Whether to output a file name instead of a full URL. Default: `false`.

use_transformations.description: Whether to include transformation segments in an output URL. Default: `true`.
use_transformations.markdownDescription: Whether to include transformation segments in an output URL. Default: `true`.

config.description: Options to be passed to Cloudinary, such as `multiple`. The `cloud_name` and `api_key` options are required for the global `media_library` option. See the [Cloudinary documentation](https://cloudinary.com/documentation/media_library_widget#2_set_the_configuration_options) for a full list of available options. Some options, including `inline_container`, will be ignored in Sveltia CMS because we use an API-based integration instead of Cloudinary’s pre-built widget.
config.markdownDescription: Options to be passed to Cloudinary, such as `multiple`. The `cloud_name` and `api_key` options are required for the global `media_library` option. See the [Cloudinary documentation](https://cloudinary.com/documentation/media_library_widget#2_set_the_configuration_options) for a full list of available options. Some options, including `inline_container`, will be ignored in Sveltia CMS because we use an API-based integration instead of Cloudinary’s pre-built widget.

config.description: Options to be passed to Uploadcare, such as `multiple`. The `publicKey` option is required for the global `media_library` option. See the [Uploadcare documentation](https://uploadcare.com/docs/uploads/file-uploader-options/) for a full list of available options. Some options, including `previewStep`, will be ignored in Sveltia CMS because we use an API-based integration instead of Uploadcare’s deprecated jQuery File Uploader.
config.markdownDescription: Options to be passed to Uploadcare, such as `multiple`. The `publicKey` option is required for the global `media_library` option. See the [Uploadcare documentation](https://uploadcare.com/docs/uploads/file-uploader-options/) for a full list of available options. Some options, including `previewStep`, will be ignored in Sveltia CMS because we use an API-based integration instead of Uploadcare’s deprecated jQuery File Uploader.

name.description: Media library name (used when configuring via legacy `media_library`).
name.markdownDescription: Media library name (used when configuring via legacy `media_library`).

access_key_id.description: AWS access key ID or equivalent (safe to store in config).
access_key_id.markdownDescription: AWS access key ID or equivalent (safe to store in config).

region.description: AWS region (e.g., 'us-east-1'). Required for Amazon S3, DigitalOcean Spaces, Scaleway Object Storage, and Supabase Storage.
region.markdownDescription: AWS region (e.g., 'us-east-1'). Required for Amazon S3, DigitalOcean Spaces, Scaleway Object Storage, and Supabase Storage.

account_id.description: Cloudflare account ID. Required for Cloudflare R2.
account_id.markdownDescription: Cloudflare account ID. Required for Cloudflare R2.

jurisdiction.description: Cloudflare R2 jurisdiction. Required for buckets created in the EU or FedRAMP jurisdictions; the global endpoint returns an error for those buckets. Default: `'default'`.
jurisdiction.markdownDescription: Cloudflare R2 jurisdiction. Required for buckets created in the EU or FedRAMP jurisdictions; the global endpoint returns an error for those buckets. Default: `'default'`.

project_id.description: Supabase project reference ID. Required for Supabase Storage.
project_id.markdownDescription: Supabase project reference ID. Required for Supabase Storage.

endpoint.description: Custom endpoint URL for S3-compatible services.
endpoint.markdownDescription: Custom endpoint URL for S3-compatible services.

force_path_style.description: Use path-style URLs instead of virtual-hosted-style.
force_path_style.markdownDescription: Use path-style URLs instead of virtual-hosted-style.

public_url.description: Base URL for public asset access. When set, asset preview and download URLs are constructed as `{public_url}/{key}` instead of the S3 API endpoint URL. Required for Cloudflare R2 (S3 API endpoint always requires authentication); set to the `r2.dev` development URL (e.g. `https://pub-abcd1234.r2.dev`) or a custom domain. Optional for Amazon S3 and DigitalOcean Spaces — use when serving assets through a CDN or custom domain (e.g. CloudFront or Route 53 for S3, CDN endpoint for Spaces).
public_url.markdownDescription: Base URL for public asset access. When set, asset preview and download URLs are constructed as `{public_url}/{key}` instead of the S3 API endpoint URL. Required for Cloudflare R2 (S3 API endpoint always requires authentication); set to the `r2.dev` development URL (e.g. `https://pub-abcd1234.r2.dev`) or a custom domain. Optional for Amazon S3 and DigitalOcean Spaces — use when serving assets through a CDN or custom domain (e.g. CloudFront or Route 53 for S3, CDN endpoint for Spaces).

providers.description: Enabled stock photo/video providers. The stock photo/video section in the asset browser is hidden if an empty array is given. Default: all supported providers.
providers.markdownDescription: Enabled stock photo/video providers. The stock photo/video section in the asset browser is hidden if an empty array is given. Default: all supported providers.

StockMediaLibrary.description: Options for the unified stock photo/video providers.
StockMediaLibrary.markdownDescription: Options for the unified stock photo/video providers.

src.description: Absolute URL or absolute path to the site logo that will be displayed on the entrance page and the browser’s tab (favicon). A square image works best.
src.markdownDescription: Absolute URL or absolute path to the site logo that will be displayed on the entrance page and the browser’s tab (favicon). A square image works best.

show_in_header.description: Whether to show the logo in the header. Default: `true`.
show_in_header.markdownDescription: Whether to show the logo in the header. Default: `true`.

url.description: URL of the issue reporting endpoint. Default: `https://github.com/sveltia/sveltia-cms/issues/new`.
url.markdownDescription: URL of the issue reporting endpoint. Default: `https://github.com/sveltia/sveltia-cms/issues/new`.

sanitize_replacement.description: String to replace sanitized characters. Default: `-`.
sanitize_replacement.markdownDescription: String to replace sanitized characters. Default: `-`.

maxlength.description: The maximum number of characters allowed for an entry slug. Default: `Infinity`.
maxlength.markdownDescription: The maximum number of characters allowed for an entry slug. Default: `Infinity`.

trim.description: Whether to trim leading and trailing replacement characters. Default: `true`.
trim.markdownDescription: Whether to trim leading and trailing replacement characters. Default: `true`.

lowercase.description: Whether to convert the slug to lowercase. Default: `true`.
lowercase.markdownDescription: Whether to convert the slug to lowercase. Default: `true`.

timezone.description: Timezone to be used for date-based slug template tags, such as `{{day}}` and `{{hour}}`. Default is `utc` for backward compatibility with Netlify/Decap CMS. Use `local` to generate slugs based on the local time of the user’s browser, which is more intuitive in most cases.
timezone.markdownDescription: Timezone to be used for date-based slug template tags, such as `{{day}}` and `{{hour}}`. Default is `utc` for backward compatibility with Netlify/Decap CMS. Use `local` to generate slugs based on the local time of the user’s browser, which is more intuitive in most cases.

folder.description: Base folder path relative to the project root. It can contain slashes to create subfolders.
folder.markdownDescription: Base folder path relative to the project root. It can contain slashes to create subfolders.

fields.description: Set of fields to be included in entries.
fields.markdownDescription: Set of fields to be included in entries.

path.description: File path relative to `folder`, without a file extension. It can contain slashes to create subfolders. Default: `{{slug}}`. To use Hugo’s page bundle, set this to `{{slug}}/index`.
path.markdownDescription: File path relative to `folder`, without a file extension. It can contain slashes to create subfolders. Default: `{{slug}}`. To use Hugo’s page bundle, set this to `{{slug}}/index`.

create.description: Whether to allow users to create entries in the collection. Default: `true`. Note that the default value is `false` in Netlify/Decap CMS, whereas Sveltia CMS sets it to `true` to provide a better out-of-the-box experience.
create.markdownDescription: Whether to allow users to create entries in the collection. Default: `true`. Note that the default value is `false` in Netlify/Decap CMS, whereas Sveltia CMS sets it to `true` to provide a better out-of-the-box experience.

delete.description: Whether to allow users to delete entries in the collection. Default: `true`.
delete.markdownDescription: Whether to allow users to delete entries in the collection. Default: `true`.

duplicate.description: Whether to allow users to duplicate entries in the collection. Default: `true`.
duplicate.markdownDescription: Whether to allow users to duplicate entries in the collection. Default: `true`.

reorder.description: Whether to allow users to reorder entries in the collection. Default: `false`. If set to `true`, entries can be reordered with a drag-and-drop UI, and the numeric order starting from 1 is saved in an automatically generated `order` field. If an object with a `key` property is provided, e.g. `{ key: 'weight' }`, the specified field is used to save the order instead of the default `order` field.
reorder.markdownDescription: Whether to allow users to reorder entries in the collection. Default: `false`. If set to `true`, entries can be reordered with a drag-and-drop UI, and the numeric order starting from 1 is saved in an automatically generated `order` field. If an object with a `key` property is provided, e.g. `{ key: 'weight' }`, the specified field is used to save the order instead of the default `order` field.

identifier_field.description: Field name to be used as the title and slug of an entry. Default: `title`.
identifier_field.markdownDescription: Field name to be used as the title and slug of an entry. Default: `title`.

slug.description: Item slug template. Default: `identifier_field` option value. It cannot contain slashes; to organize entries in subfolders, use the `path` option instead. It’s possible to [localize the slug](https://sveltiacms.app/en/docs/i18n#localizing-entry-slugs) or [use a random ID](https://sveltiacms.app/en/docs/collections/entries#slug-template-tags). Also, it’s possible to show a special slug editor field in initial entry drafts by using `{{fields._slug}}` (with an underscore prefix) or `{{fields._slug | localize}}` (to localize the slug).
slug.markdownDescription: Item slug template. Default: `identifier_field` option value. It cannot contain slashes; to organize entries in subfolders, use the `path` option instead. It’s possible to [localize the slug](https://sveltiacms.app/en/docs/i18n#localizing-entry-slugs) or [use a random ID](https://sveltiacms.app/en/docs/collections/entries#slug-template-tags). Also, it’s possible to show a special slug editor field in initial entry drafts by using `{{fields._slug}}` (with an underscore prefix) or `{{fields._slug | localize}}` (to localize the slug).

slug_length.description: The maximum number of characters allowed for an entry slug. Default: `Infinity`.
slug_length.markdownDescription: The maximum number of characters allowed for an entry slug. Default: `Infinity`.

sortable_fields.description: Custom sortable fields. Default: `title`, `name`, `date`, `author` and `description`. For a Git backend, commit author and commit date are also included by default. See the [documentation](https://sveltiacms.app/en/docs/collections/entries#sorting) for details.
sortable_fields.markdownDescription: Custom sortable fields. Default: `title`, `name`, `date`, `author` and `description`. For a Git backend, commit author and commit date are also included by default. See the [documentation](https://sveltiacms.app/en/docs/collections/entries#sorting) for details.

view_filters.description: View filters to be used in the entry list.
view_filters.markdownDescription: View filters to be used in the entry list.

view_groups.description: View groups to be used in the entry list.
view_groups.markdownDescription: View groups to be used in the entry list.

nested.description: Options for a nested collection. Note that nested collections are not yet supported in Sveltia CMS.
nested.markdownDescription: Options for a nested collection. Note that nested collections are not yet supported in Sveltia CMS.

meta.description: Meta data for a nested collection. Note that nested collections are not yet supported in Sveltia CMS.
meta.markdownDescription: Meta data for a nested collection. Note that nested collections are not yet supported in Sveltia CMS.

index_file.description: Index file inclusion options. If `true`, the default index file name is `_index`, which is used for Hugo’s special index file. See the [documentation](https://sveltiacms.app/en/docs/collections/entries#managing-hugo-s-special-index-file) for details.
index_file.markdownDescription: Index file inclusion options. If `true`, the default index file name is `_index`, which is used for Hugo’s special index file. See the [documentation](https://sveltiacms.app/en/docs/collections/entries#managing-hugo-s-special-index-file) for details.

thumbnail.description: Whether to show entry thumbnails in the entry list. Default: `true` (auto-detect image/file fields). Set to `false` to disable, or provide a field key path (e.g., `heroImage.src`) or an array of paths for fallbacks. Supports nested fields with dot notation and wildcards (e.g., `images.*.src`). An empty array equals `false`.
thumbnail.markdownDescription: Whether to show entry thumbnails in the entry list. Default: `true` (auto-detect image/file fields). Set to `false` to disable, or provide a field key path (e.g., `heroImage.src`) or an array of paths for fallbacks. Supports nested fields with dot notation and wildcards (e.g., `images.*.src`). An empty array equals `false`.

limit.description: The maximum number of entries that can be created in the collection. Default: `Infinity`.
limit.markdownDescription: The maximum number of entries that can be created in the collection. Default: `Infinity`.

label_singular.description: Singular UI label. It will be Blog Post if the `label` is Blog Posts, for example. Default: `label` option value.
label_singular.markdownDescription: Singular UI label. It will be Blog Post if the `label` is Blog Posts, for example. Default: `label` option value.

description.description: Short description of the collection to be displayed in the editor UI.
description.markdownDescription: Short description of the collection to be displayed in the editor UI.

media_folder.description: Internal media folder path for the collection. This overrides the global `media_folder` option. It can be a relative path from the project root if it starts with a slash. Otherwise it’s a path relative to the entry. If this option is omitted, the global `media_folder` option value is used. See the [documentation](https://sveltiacms.app/en/docs/media/internal#collection-level-configuration) for details.
media_folder.markdownDescription: Internal media folder path for the collection. This overrides the global `media_folder` option. It can be a relative path from the project root if it starts with a slash. Otherwise it’s a path relative to the entry. If this option is omitted, the global `media_folder` option value is used. See the [documentation](https://sveltiacms.app/en/docs/media/internal#collection-level-configuration) for details.

public_folder.description: Public media folder path for an entry collection. This overrides the global `public_folder` option. Default: `media_folder` option value.
public_folder.markdownDescription: Public media folder path for an entry collection. This overrides the global `public_folder` option. Default: `media_folder` option value.

hide.description: Whether to hide the collection in the UI. Default: `false`.
hide.markdownDescription: Whether to hide the collection in the UI. Default: `false`.

publish.description: Whether to show the publishing control UI for Editorial Workflow. Default: `true`. Note that Editorial Workflow is not yet supported in Sveltia CMS.
publish.markdownDescription: Whether to show the publishing control UI for Editorial Workflow. Default: `true`. Note that Editorial Workflow is not yet supported in Sveltia CMS.

format.description: File format. It should match the file extension. Default: `yaml-frontmatter`.
format.markdownDescription: File format. It should match the file extension. Default: `yaml-frontmatter`.

frontmatter_delimiter.description: Delimiters to be used for the front matter format. Default: depends on the front matter type.
frontmatter_delimiter.markdownDescription: Delimiters to be used for the front matter format. Default: depends on the front matter type.

body_field.description: Body field options for front matter formats.
body_field.markdownDescription: Body field options for front matter formats.

i18n.description: I18n options. Default: `false`.
i18n.markdownDescription: I18n options. Default: `false`.

yaml_quote.description: Whether to double-quote all the strings values if the YAML format is used for file output. Default: `false`.
yaml_quote.markdownDescription: Whether to double-quote all the strings values if the YAML format is used for file output. Default: `false`.
yaml_quote.deprecationMessage: Use the global YAML format options. `yaml_quote: true` is equivalent to `quote: double`. See the documentation https://sveltiacms.app/en/docs/data-output#controlling-data-output for details.

name.description: Unique identifier for the collection.
name.markdownDescription: Unique identifier for the collection.

label.description: Label of the field to be displayed in the editor UI. Default: `name` option value.
label.markdownDescription: Label of the field to be displayed in the editor UI. Default: `name` option value.

icon.description: Name of a [Material Symbols icon](https://fonts.google.com/icons?icon.set=Material+Symbols) to be displayed in the collection list.
icon.markdownDescription: Name of a [Material Symbols icon](https://fonts.google.com/icons?icon.set=Material+Symbols) to be displayed in the collection list.

EntryCollection.markdownDescription: Entry collection definition. In Netlify/Decap CMS, an entry collection is called a folder collection.

StandardField.description: Entry field using a built-in field type.
StandardField.markdownDescription: Entry field using a built-in field type.

before_input.description: An extra label to be displayed before the input UI. Markdown is supported. Default: empty string.
before_input.markdownDescription: An extra label to be displayed before the input UI. Markdown is supported. Default: empty string.

after_input.description: An extra label to be displayed after the input UI. Markdown is supported. Default: empty string.
after_input.markdownDescription: An extra label to be displayed after the input UI. Markdown is supported. Default: empty string.

default.description: Default value. Accepts `true` or `false`.
default.markdownDescription: Default value. Accepts `true` or `false`.

label.description: Label of the field to be displayed in the editor UI. Default: `name` field value.
label.markdownDescription: Label of the field to be displayed in the editor UI. Default: `name` field value.

comment.description: Short description of the field to be displayed in the editor UI.
comment.markdownDescription: Short description of the field to be displayed in the editor UI.

hint.description: Help message to be displayed below the input UI. Limited Markdown formatting is supported: bold, italic, strikethrough and links.
hint.markdownDescription: Help message to be displayed below the input UI. Limited Markdown formatting is supported: bold, italic, strikethrough and links.

preview.description: Whether to show the preview of the field. Default: `true`.
preview.markdownDescription: Whether to show the preview of the field. Default: `true`.

required.description: Whether to make data input on the field required. Default: `true`. This option also affects data output if the `omit_empty_optional_fields` global output option is `true`. If i18n is enabled and the field doesn’t require input in all locales, required locale codes can be passed as an array like `[en, fr]` instead of a boolean.
required.markdownDescription: Whether to make data input on the field required. Default: `true`. readonly.description: Whether to make the field read-only. Default: `false`. This is useful when a `default` value is provided and the field should not be editable by users.
readonly.markdownDescription: Whether to make the field read-only. Default: `false`. This is useful when a `default` value is provided and the field should not be editable by users.

name.description: Unique identifier for the field. It cannot include periods and spaces.
name.markdownDescription: Unique identifier for the field. It cannot include periods and spaces.

i18n.description: Whether to enable the editor UI in locales other than the default locale. Default: `false`. `duplicate` disables the UI in non-default like `false` but automatically copies the default locale’s value to other locales. `translate` and `none` are aliases of `true` and `false`, respectively. This option only works when i18n is set up with the global and collection-level `i18n` option. See the [documentation](https://sveltiacms.app/en/docs/i18n#field-level-configuration) for details.
i18n.markdownDescription: Whether to enable the editor UI in locales other than the default locale. default.description: Default value. It must be a string if `output_code_only` is `false`. Otherwise it must be an object that match the `keys` option.
default.markdownDescription: Default value. It must be a string if `output_code_only` is `false`. Otherwise it must be an object that match the `keys` option.

default_language.description: Default language to be selected, like `js`. See the [Prism documentation](https://prismjs.com/#supported-languages) for a list of supported languages. Default: empty string, which is plaintext.
default_language.markdownDescription: Default language to be selected, like `js`. See the [Prism documentation](https://prismjs.com/#supported-languages) for a list of supported languages. Default: empty string, which is plaintext.

allow_language_selection.markdownDescription: Whether to show a language switcher so that users can change the language mode. Default: `true` (the Decap CMS document is wrong).

output_code_only.markdownDescription: Whether to output code snippet only. Default: `false`.

keys.description: Output property names. It has no effect if `output_code_only` is `true`. Default: `{ code: 'code', lang: 'lang' }`.
keys.markdownDescription: Output property names. It has no effect if `output_code_only` is `true`. Default: `{ code: 'code', lang: 'lang' }`.

default.description: Default value. Accepts a date/time string that matches the `format`, or `{{now}}` to populate the current date/time. Default: empty string.
default.markdownDescription: Default value. Accepts a date/time string that matches the `format`, or `{{now}}` to populate the current date/time. Default: empty string.

type.description: The [`type`](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Reference/Elements/input#input_types) HTML attribute value for the date/time input. If `type` is set to `date`, the input will only accept date values and the time part will be disabled. If `type` is set to `time`, the input will only accept time values and the date part will be disabled. Default: `datetime-local`, which accepts both date and time values.
type.markdownDescription: The [`type`](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Reference/Elements/input#input_types) HTML attribute value for the date/time input. If `type` is set to `date`, the input will only accept date values and the time part will be disabled. If `type` is set to `time`, the input will only accept time values and the date part will be disabled. Default: `datetime-local`, which accepts both date and time values.

min.description: The [`min`](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Reference/Attributes/min) HTML attribute value for the date/time input. The expected format depends on the `type` option: `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm` for `datetime-local`, `YYYY-MM-DD` for `date`, and `HH:mm` for `time`.
min.markdownDescription: The [`min`](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Reference/Attributes/min) HTML attribute value for the date/time input. The expected format depends on the `type` option: `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm` for `datetime-local`, `YYYY-MM-DD` for `date`, and `HH:mm` for `time`.

max.description: The [`max`](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Reference/Attributes/max) HTML attribute value for the date/time input. The expected format depends on the `type` option: `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm` for `datetime-local`, `YYYY-MM-DD` for `date`, and `HH:mm` for `time`.
max.markdownDescription: The [`max`](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Reference/Attributes/max) HTML attribute value for the date/time input. The expected format depends on the `type` option: `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm` for `datetime-local`, `YYYY-MM-DD` for `date`, and `HH:mm` for `time`.

step.description: The [`step`](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Reference/Attributes/step) HTML attribute value for the date/time input. Accepts a positive integer or `'any'`. For `datetime-local` and `time` inputs, the integer represents the step in seconds (e.g. `300` for 5-minute steps). For `date` inputs, the integer represents the step in days (e.g. `7` for weekly steps). Default: `60` seconds for `datetime-local` and `time`; `1` day for `date`.
step.markdownDescription: The [`step`](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Reference/Attributes/step) HTML attribute value for the date/time input. Accepts a positive integer or `'any'`. For `datetime-local` and `time` inputs, the integer represents the step in seconds (e.g. `300` for 5-minute steps). For `date` inputs, the integer represents the step in days (e.g. `7` for weekly steps). Default: `60` seconds for `datetime-local` and `time`; `1` day for `date`.

format.description: Storage format written in [Day.js tokens](https://day.js.org/docs/en/display/format). Default: ISO 8601 format.
format.markdownDescription: Storage format written in [Day.js tokens](https://day.js.org/docs/en/display/format). Default: ISO 8601 format.

date_format.description: Date storage format written in [Day.js tokens](https://day.js.org/docs/en/display/format) if the value is a string and the `format` option is not defined. If `true`, ISO 8601 format is used unless the `format` option is defined. If `false`, date input/output is disabled. This option is available for backward compatibility with Netlify CMS; use the `format` or `type` option instead.

picker_utc.description: Whether to make the date input/output UTC. Default: `false`. This option is available for backward compatibility with Netlify/Decap CMS. The newer `input_timezone` and `output_utc` options provide more flexibility and supersede this option when explicitly set. `picker_utc: true` is equivalent to `input_timezone: 'utc'`.
picker_utc.markdownDescription: Whether to make the date input/output UTC. Default: `false`. This option is available for backward compatibility with Netlify/Decap CMS. The newer `input_timezone` and `output_utc` options provide more flexibility and supersede this option when explicitly set. `picker_utc: true` is equivalent to `input_timezone: 'utc'`.

input_timezone.description: Timezone used by the date/time input. This option supersedes `picker_utc`. If set to `local`, the browser’s local timezone is used. If set to `utc`, UTC is used. A custom IANA timezone name such as `America/New_York` or `Asia/Tokyo` may also be provided as a string. Default: `local`.
input_timezone.markdownDescription: Timezone used by the date/time input. This option supersedes `picker_utc`. If set to `local`, the browser’s local timezone is used. If set to `utc`, UTC is used. A custom IANA timezone name such as `America/New_York` or `Asia/Tokyo` may also be provided as a string. Default: `local`.

output_utc.description: Whether to convert stored values to UTC. This option supersedes `picker_utc`. If `false`, output values preserve the timezone semantics of `input_timezone`: `local` omits timezone information, `utc` appends a `Z` suffix, and custom timezones preserve their offset (e.g., `-05:00`). If `true`, the input value is converted to UTC for storage. When no custom `format` is specified, a `Z` suffix is appended to the ISO 8601 output. When a custom `format` is used, the value is stored in UTC but formatted according to that pattern — which won’t include an explicit timezone indicator unless the format itself contains `Z`. Note that `input_timezone: 'utc'` already implies UTC semantics, so `output_utc` has no additional effect in that case. Default: `false`.

encode_file_path.markdownDescription: Whether to encode the file path in File/Image fields. Default: `false`. This is useful when a file path contains special characters that need to be URL-encoded, such as spaces and parentheses. For example, `Hello World (1).webp` would be `Hello%20World%20%281%29.webp`. In general, File/Image fields should contain the original file path, and web-specific encoding should be done in the front-end code.

sanitize_preview.description: Whether to sanitize the preview HTML. Default: `true`. Note that Sveltia CMS has changed the default value from `false` to `true` to enhance security, whereas Netlify/Decap CMS keeps it as `false`. We recommend keeping this option enabled unless disabling it fixes a broken preview and you fully trust all users of your CMS.
sanitize_preview.markdownDescription: Whether to sanitize the preview HTML. Default: `true`. Note that Sveltia CMS has changed the default value from `false` to `true` to enhance security, whereas Netlify/Decap CMS keeps it as `false`. We recommend keeping this option enabled unless disabling it fixes a broken preview and you fully trust all users of your CMS.

linked_images.description: Whether to enable the linked images feature for the built-in `image` component. Default: `true`. When enabled, the image component provides an additional text field for specifying a URL to wrap the image as a link. The resulting Markdown output will be in the format `[![alt](src)](link)`, where clicking the image navigates to the provided link. This feature can be disabled if it causes conflicts with certain frameworks.
